mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for simplicity and speed, ideal for development, IoT, and edge computing. It provides a fully conformant Kubernetes experience with minimal overhead, making it suitable for local development environments and CI/CD pipelines. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to full Kubernetes distributions and reliance on the Snap package manager.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using Snap, which is the recommended method for installation on Linux systems.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo by adding it to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

newgrp microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your actual username if needed.
> Refresh the group membership in the current session.

steps.title: Verify MicroK8s Installation
steps.description:
This step checks that MicroK8s is installed correctly and that the Kubernetes cluster is running.

```bash

microk8s status --wait-ready

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Waits until MicroK8s is fully initialized.
> Verifies that the node is ready.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential Kubernetes add-ons like DNS and the dashboard for better management.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables the DNS add-on for service discovery.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for UI management.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This will remove MicroK8s and all associated data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

microk8s stop

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s start

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Stops the MicroK8s service before upgrading.
> Upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.
> Starts MicroK8s after the upgrade.
> Verifies that the nodes are in a ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not installed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s has not been installed or the Snap package manager is not available.

**Fix:**
Install MicroK8s using 'sudo snap install microk8s --classic'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command is not available in the path.

**Fix:**
Use 'microk8s kubectl' instead of 'kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s kubectl config view --raw > $HOME/.kube/config' to set up the kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s status shows 'not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s services are not started.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s start' to start the services.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, including improved RBAC and enhanced metrics server.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy add-ons; users are encouraged to use the new add-on management system.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure all add-ons are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' when running 'microk8s kubectl get nodes'. The node should be listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for running MicroK8s.
